query: our internal/ dir has grown into a mess — there's a sandbox folder, a sandboxutil folder next to it, and nix code buried three levels deep; reorganize it so it stops feeling like spaghetti
rationale: direct: reorganize a tangled tree into clean axes, misplaced util/nix packages

query: I have isolation, network and provision as separate flags but the folders don't reflect that — where should the nix provisioning code actually live?
rationale: direct: structure should mirror the orthogonal axes; nixprov belongs under a provision axis

query: we have bwrap and docker backends and want to add a third without editing the existing ones — how should the packages be laid out?
rationale: implicit: shared-core + per-variant leaves, Strategy per axis, open-for-extension layout

query: name these sibling modules consistently — right now it's sandbox, net, and nixprov and it reads inconsistently
rationale: naming symmetry: sibling concepts need sibling grammar; tool-named leaves are a smell

query: the CLI and the kubernetes operator have drifted — they implement the same concepts but with totally different folder structures; make them symmetric
rationale: consumer symmetry: two realizers of the same axes should rhyme, share down to a common lib

query: bwrap needs some nix-specific glue to mount the closure — should that be a new top-level package?
rationale: variation-point bridge: localize cross-axis coupling inside the type, don't hoist a sibling

query: should this be packaged by feature or by layer? right now everything is split into controllers/, services/, utils/, types/
rationale: package-by-axis/feature vs by-layer; layer packaging hides the axes

query: split the shared code from the type-specific code in this module tree so each backend only owns its own specifics
rationale: commonality/variability: shared kernel vs per-variant leaf

query: restructure this 96-byte particle struct into SoA so the update loop stops missing cache
rationale: near-miss: runtime data layout for cache -> data-oriented-design-guide, not module structure

query: should this be a class with inheritance or composition of functions — design the object model
rationale: near-miss: class-vs-function design -> oop-guide / fp-guide

query: set up moon task inheritance so projects tagged skill auto-pick-up the build task
rationale: near-miss: monorepo task config -> moon-guide, not source-tree decomposition

query: I'm designing a new tool with three independent config dimensions — input format, transport, and output sink — how do I lay out the packages so adding one input doesn't touch the others?
rationale: validation positive: orthogonal axes + cross-product, fan-out layout, open-for-extension

query: a single utils package at the repo root has become a dumping ground — every axis throws its helpers in there; how should I distribute them?
rationale: validation positive: per-axis shared/, axis owns its own utils, no homeless util sibling

query: rename the provisioner type from nixprov to just nix and move it next to its sibling provisioners
rationale: validation positive: bare type names under axis context, sibling co-location

query: write the kubernetes Deployment and NetworkPolicy manifests for this service
rationale: validation negative: authoring k8s manifests -> kubernetes-guide, not package decomposition

query: profile whether the broad-phase is cache-bound before I change any layout
rationale: validation negative: measurement of runtime cache behavior -> data-oriented-design-guide

query: adding a new storage backend means editing a giant switch in three files — make it so a new backend plugs in without touching the core
rationale: near-miss: open/closed plugin extension -> microkernel-pattern-guide

query: design a plugin registry so each provider registers itself and the core never imports the concrete implementations
rationale: near-miss: plugin registry / open-closed -> microkernel-pattern-guide

query: this Isolator interface has nine methods and half the implementations just return nil — split it so each type only implements what it needs
rationale: near-miss: narrow ports / interface segregation -> hexagonal-pattern-guide

query: the core package imports the concrete docker and nix packages directly; invert it so the core depends only on interfaces
rationale: near-miss: dependency inversion / ports-and-adapters -> hexagonal-pattern-guide

query: should I model this as an abstract base class with virtual methods or a plain interface — which OO construct fits?
rationale: near-miss: class/paradigm construct choice -> oop-guide, not axis decomposition

query: logging, metrics, and the auth check are copied into every backend and I keep forgetting one — where should concerns that touch every backend live?
rationale: cross-cutting concerns: scattering/tangling, weave once at the composition root, not a sibling axis

query: should the storage backend be picked at compile time with build flags, or at runtime from config — and how does that change the wiring?
rationale: near-miss: binding time -> microkernel-pattern-guide

query: we split the monolith into packages but everything still imports everything — how do I actually enforce the module boundaries?
rationale: false modularity / distributed monolith: enforce with import/architecture tests + data ownership, not folders

query: grade how tightly these two modules are coupled and tell me concretely how to loosen the worst part
rationale: near-miss: coupling vocabulary -> connascence-guide

query: two teams keep colliding on the same module — how should we draw the boundary so each owns its piece?
rationale: boundary alignment: Conway's law, single owner per axis, domain seams

query: write the actual bubblewrap unshare/bind syscall sequence for the sandbox runner
rationale: near-miss: implementing one variant's internals, not deciding the decomposition
